author: Ricky
msg:
One of my ULOs is facing a strange issue. When applying a new lease code on a device, he gets an “attestation error.” However, if he reverts to a lease code that was previously running on the same device, the Unity app works fine.

As per our understanding, an attestation error is related to device integrity. In that case, it’s unclear how reverting to an older lease still works.

He has tested this across multiple phones and email IDs, and the issue persists—but only on a few specific devices.
One of such device is Motorola Fusion+ running on android 11
Has anyone else faced this, or could the team please check if this is a bug?  @Kyle_FrenOne @unityassistant  pl help on this too

author: Ricky
msg:
Follow up reply from team

We appreciate the thoroughness, but we're not able to share the specifics of the attestation architecture — including which levels are applied, how they map to leases or user profiles,
  and the internal flagging criteria. This is by design, as publishing these details would undermine the security measures the system is built on.

  What we can confirm is that the behaviour you're seeing — older lease working, newer lease failing on the same device — is expected in certain cases. Attestation requirements are not
  static and can differ based on multiple factors that are not tied solely to the device.

  For the affected devices, the recommendation remains the same: **make sure they are not rooted, have a locked bootloader, and are running stock firmware** with up-to-date Google Play
  Services. The alternative attestation pathway currently in development will broaden device compatibility.

author: Hattori Hanzo
msg:
If any UNO is looking for a place to easely share licenses at any prefered setting. 
 
You get a dedicated URL where ULO can claim a license easy in a few steps.
Upload leascodes in bulk by sharing a csv, each claim wil pick a new unclaimed license from your dedicated pool.

Currently there is a trial where a few can sign up for. Application via info@unityrewards.io

author: Wopdiddly
msg:
I don't think it's awful, ten cents. I'm also wondering if the reward is time based as the one of the three CLI I caught the fastest paid 20c.

Anyway if you had all 200 leased on this one task alone at 50/50 and every one got one CLI. That's a 73% ROI on CLI alone, over a year,  before telemetry and everything else to come.

I can see how this will all work long term, it's just really hard to be patient and ofc there are other well versed historical issues but you can start to see the long term potential.
